reviews.review_title: Updated Review in response to Developer
reviews.review_description:
This is an excellent Mixed reality arena shooter with very appealing neon lit, retro arcade graphics and a gorgeous retro soundtrack. I'm very happy that this game includes a solo mode that can be played in an arena with bots which do pose a reasonable challenge. 

But it's held back by one.really frustrating and annoying problem. The requirement to manually set up your play space. Please please please add the option to use meta room scans! 

Manually setting up your room is such a chore. Only further exacerbated by the fact that unless you recalibrate the floor in the correct way it spawns the room out of sync with its anchor. And there's no indication of how to properly calibrate the floor to avoid this. This is what held back my review of only two stars. Otherwise it would be four or five.

I'm updating this review in response to the developers who kindly responded to me. 

I understand that the meta scan does not work with large spaces but then surely the solution until they resolve. This is to give those who want to plain small space is the option of using metiscan with an appropriate warning that it may not work with larger spaces.

developer_response.body:
Hi Martbolshy17,

Thank you so much for your detailed review! We really appreciate your feedback.

We completely understand your frustration with manually setting up the play space. We use our own mapping and anchor system instead of Meta’s Room Scan because Laser Limbo is designed to work in both small and very large spaces. Unfortunately, Meta’s Room Scan currently doesn’t support larger areas reliably. In bigger rooms, the tracked space can sometimes shift, which is why we need a dependable method to recalibrate the maps.

We hear you loud and clear, though! As soon as Meta’s tracking works reliably in larger spaces, we’ll gladly integrate it as an option to make setup much easier.

Thanks again for your feedback and for playing Laser Limbo!

reviews.review_description:
I'm currently playing solo, but my family can't wait to come over and join in. I don't mind the calibration process at all—the payoff makes it totally worth it.

​To make things even more fun, I’d love to see more chaos: bigger guns, more explosions, better effects, and a wider variety of enemies. The holes you can blast in the ceiling are amazing, but make them even bigger—let the whole room collapse!

​This game got me hooked immediately; I went straight to Discord to follow the development. I can't wait to see what 2026 brings. This team is sitting on a goldmine.

developer_response.body: Thank so much for your great review and your inspiring feedback - I hope we can implement it at one point. Let's maybe chat on discount about details :)

reviews.review_description:
First of all... I want to know why on earth, this game didn't just use the space that you can set up for mixed reality games. I'm sorry but the fact that not only does this game require you to make a whole new space that's dedicated to this one game, meanwhile in literally every single game that features mixed reality, only goes off of whatever space you make in the meta Quest menu... It's somehow more annoying.

I don't understand the point of having to mark a line on the floor at the very beginning... And the game doesn't even ask you where the floor is. You have to answer that question TWICE. 

I also don't understand why you need to mark the corners of your room FROM THE CEILING as opposed to the meta Quest space where you mark the floor level ONCE, You of course mark where the ceiling is, and then you generate the walls, by just stretching out your virtual wall without needing to look at the ceiling in order to do so.

If setting up the play space made sense, the actual application would have just started off with setting up the items. Tidal tactics did it, home invasion did it etc.  

When I just got to setting up the items, setting up the score board, EASY. Ok Why do I have to set up 2 team spawn points? And it's not like the game told you "set up a green spawn point and a red spawn point" the game just told me to set up a spawn point for each team. Doesn't tell me HOW MANY TEAMS, Doesn't tell me WHAT COLOR THE TEAMS ARE, Nothing! I put down the green spawn point and then when I saw a pink spawn point, I assumed that it was pink because the game wouldn't allow me to spawn it there! So I'm just spawning green spawn points all over my room, being like "HOW MANY TEAMS DO YOU THINK I NEED!" Only to find out that "OH! The pink team is the only other team aside from the green one! THANKS GAME FOR NOT SPECIFYING THAT TO ME!"

And even when you are setting up your room, you aren't given the chance to reverse your decision for how many corners or how high the walls are, so if you have controllers that don't track very well, you're stuck with the bad angles until you start all over again.

Another question I have is what's the point of having to set up TWO spawn points when the vr headset you're using is meant for 1 person? What I mean by this, is the other spawn point that you're forced to set up is just a pixel cluster that's just useless. It's not like you're gonna connect with a player and they're just gonna automatically sync your room with theirs a, because chances are, they have their own headset with a COMPLETELY DIFFERENT SETUP! It really just feels like the game is padding itself out before you actually play the game.

So after going through that monstrosity of having to set up your guardian and your play space, you FINALLY get to play the game. To be honest, I am so glad that I captured this game when it was free because THESE GAMES ARE BORING AF!

Most of the games found in the "host" menu are comprised of games that require multiplayer in order for them to be fun. You could still play them single player but not having a second person to play with, will make the game as boring as possible. And most of these games really are just "grab the coins and put them in the piggy bank before the other one can. Or pop the bubbles." The most bare bones stuff imaginable. Really nothing to write home about.

If this game is your first vr game in general, INCLUDING VRCHAT AND BEAT SABER, I can see the appeal but that’s it.

The only thing you can really do in single player that's MEANT for single player is this "destroy the drones" mode where you can grab weapons or a light saber and just destroy the machines. THAT'S IT! For $10 you basically get a diet version of what is basically space pirate trainer but in mixed reality- OH AND YOU CAN SET UP A LASER FOR NO REASON!

Unless you absolutely want to play with a friend, look elsewhere, space pirate trainer not only is a better drone swarm shooter, but the multiplayer games are much more engaging than this.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for investing so much time in this review  :)

I could explain why we don't use Metas room scan, but I think it doesn't make any sense if you it the game so much.

reviews.review_description: I mean it. Everyone loves it. It's like having a fancy arcade at home.

Laser tag in your house and a vr gun that doesn’t require a pc and all these other 1,000s of dollar add ons, AWESOME. But please I’m begging you, have a setting in the game for when you click the meta quest button or hold it down to record the whole room doesn’t completely shift making that room and all other created maps completely useless causing you to have to restart everything all over again. Extremely frustrating and time consuming. Lastly, please add the ability to change colors and maybe even bullet shapes. Thanks.

developer_response.body:
Hey M3tapro!
Thanks for your nice rating! However this should not happen. We actually set a spatial anchor when you are doing the two point room calibration. It should snap back after doing a recenter. Can you check if you gave Laser Limbo access to the spatial data.

Other than that you should be able to realign  your map with your room at anytime using the re-calibration function. Here you must use the very same to points and order.

Come to discord and we can talk about more detailed:
https://www.freeroam.ar/discord

reviews.review_title: So far my favorite shared space vr game
reviews.review_description:
This is usually the game I show people when they want to try vr out. Since I have 2 headsets I can set up a quick match and shoot it out.

One issue im having is that when I have one headset host a good and load it everything is fine. But as soon as the second headset joins the match the first headset loses its ability to see in passthrough, making the game much less cool and much less playable. DEVS! Any idea what the problem could be.

Thanks!

How to set a map to pass through mr mode? One room is pass through, the other is not. I can't figure out the difference. 
Different modes of the game a bit unclear and there is no explanation. 
I would love have our generation of the map based on the room scan by the headset. Shared snchy is a must.

Even just playing solo against the drones is soo good! This game really made me fall in love with augmented reality! Running around the apartment, jumping on my bed while shooting the drones - so fun!

I don’t write reviews and I barely play my VR, but ever since the introduction of mixed reality, I am so into it.  There are a few mixed reality games that are standouts. This is one of them. I’ve been searching and there not a lot. I hope the developers read this. I love that I can create a map without the spatial features. The way you have it is perfect. And I only hope you develop the solo play even more. I’m not a person to play so much with other people even though this is making want to buy a headset for every member of my family, this game is awesome.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words <3 
Yes we plan to extend the single player mode, but currently we need to focus on some multiplayer features first. We are a very small team so things aren't progressing not soo fast ^^

reviews.review_description: Beautiful experience. Thanks

reviews.review_title: FUNNIEST GAMES EVER ON META QUEST 2
reviews.review_description: I CAN PLAY THIS WITH AN ECSCLUSIVE GRUP

reviews.review_title: Very interesting but having setup issues
reviews.review_description: I look forward to trying this with other people. Is it 2, 3, 4 player? Not sure. I'm confused by obstacles like tables, counters. I can't put objects in them so the objects crowd my space. Is there an option to auto decorate walls and obstacles? I also struggled with switching/rotating objects. Lots of potential though! I'm using Quest 2

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks so much for the nice review!
Laser Limbo is limited to 3 players at the time but you can request the enterprise PC app to play with up to 8 player via Discord https://www.freeroam.ar/discord

Tables and counters etc are supposed to get a collider box (obstacle boxes) to that you can hide behind and bullets can be catcht there.

Auto map creation isn't yet available but its a futures task on our roadmap

Switching objects and rotating them can be done with the thumb sticks.

Best, Nico

reviews.review_description: I can play mixed reality on quest 2! Never had more fun playing vr in my life must have for all ages. Takes some time to set up but it is worth it!!!

reviews.review_title: Best game to play w friends
reviews.review_description: I went to hang out at a friends house and brought my quest 3 and quest 2 to play games and when we didn’t know what to play I told him about this and how it was buggy last time I tried to play it, but when I fixed it this time it was hours of nonstop fun, dodging bullets hiding behind cover and shooting back at him in real time is so much fun. Neither of us understood how this game could possible have been free, thank you so much for this experience.

As the title says.. However some issues:

- NO Quest Cloud save: After hard reset or full delete of app all rooms mappings and game setups are GONE

- Robots come from ceiling hole that is approx 40cm lower then the ceiling breaking immersion. Please fix. I remapped and reinstalled the app 3 times and every time this happened!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for review! Fair point. We haven't yet thought about Metas cloud save, but we will now.

Regarding the drone hole. This should not happen. Could you join our Discord and help us to reproduce (and fix) the problem?

Discord: https://discord.gg/T46nfPXebv

reviews.review_title: First local MR game that impresses!
reviews.review_description: With the new update that removes guardian boundaries this works great.  We turned an entire floor into MR laser tag.  This is the first MR local multiplayer game that really impressed us, great job devs

I am using Quest since 2 years, with little interest in games. Now that I have installed it, I would rate it superbly with 5 stars on account of Ease of Play, Graphics, UI and the game itself. It creates a Mesmerising Mixed Reality Play area and one can play for hours. I also have a VIVE with Space Pirate Trainer and this beats the gameplay. The map editor is fun, and Simple.
Look forward to the next update.
Hello. This cool game doesn't start at all on my Quest 3. It crashes all the time sadly. 👋🏻

developer_response.body:
Hi HairTaylor.

Can you help us find the problem by joining our Discord: https://discord.gg/T46nfPXebv

Or write me an email nico [at] freeroam [dot] ar

reviews.review_title: My favorite MR experience so far
reviews.review_description: The way you set up obstacles is very fun, love making my own maps. Can't wait to see new content

I was on the quest store and found this game. Once I downloaded lazer limbo I set on my boundary but then it required : floor wall  and  wall ceiling: I don’t know how to set that up correctly but I’ve heard great things about this game

developer_response.body: Could you pls join our Discord, so that we can help you with it? https://www.freeroam.ar/discord
